name: Safety Quotient Mesh Vocabulary
description: Shared vocabulary for the safety-quotient federated agent mesh. Schema.org base with project-specific extensions under the sqm: namespace.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Agent
hasDefinedTerm.description: An autonomous participant in the safety-quotient mesh. Each agent runs its own cognitive architecture, maintains local state, and communicates via the interagent/v1 protocol.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Autonomy Budget
hasDefinedTerm.description: Spend counter for autonomous operations. Increments per autonomous action (budget_spent). Optional cutoff (budget_cutoff) halts operation when reached; cutoff 0 means unlimited (counter-only). Wire format: autonomy_budget { budget_spent, budget_cutoff }. Formerly aliased as trust_budget (deprecated).

hasDefinedTerm.name: Epistemic Debt
hasDefinedTerm.description: Count of unresolved epistemic flags across transport messages and session summaries. Flags mark uncertainty, scope limitation, or validity threat.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Gate Status
hasDefinedTerm.description: State of a gated autonomous chain. Values: open (action proceeds), blocked (waiting for peer response), resolved (response received). Part of the gated-chains protocol extension.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Transport Method
hasDefinedTerm.description: How an agent sends and receives interagent messages. Values: git-pr (pull request delivery), cross-repo-fetch (git remote read), http (direct HTTP API).

hasDefinedTerm.name: SETL Score
hasDefinedTerm.description: Subjective Expected Truth Loss — a float [0, 1] measuring the editorial-to-structural inferential distance of a message. Lower values indicate more direct observation; higher values indicate more interpretation.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Schema Version
hasDefinedTerm.description: Integer version of the state.db schema (scripts/schema.sql). Increments on every migration. Consensus domain — changes require peer agreement.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Mesh Health
hasDefinedTerm.description: Aggregate health indicator for the federated mesh, derived from the weakest-link autonomy budget across all peers. Values: healthy (all budgets above 50%), degraded (any budget 10-50%), critical (any budget below 10% or agent unreachable).

hasDefinedTerm.name: Consensus Round
hasDefinedTerm.description: A multi-phase agreement process among mesh peers. Three tiers: C1 (HTTP mechanical, zero cost), C2 (claude -p reasoning, 2 credits/agent), C3 (human escalation). Follows PBFT 3-phase pattern: propose, vote, commit.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Consensus Vote
hasDefinedTerm.description: A peer's structured response to a consensus proposal. Values: agree, disagree, abstain, escalate. Disagree votes MAY include a counter-proposal.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Consensus Tier
hasDefinedTerm.description: Which consensus mechanism handles a shared state change. C1: HTTP mechanical (additive, deterministic). C2: claude -p reasoning (substantive). C3: human escalation (deadlock, security).

hasDefinedTerm.name: Agent Endpoint
hasDefinedTerm.description: A bounded-context HTTP endpoint served by a mesh agent. Each endpoint returns a focused JSON payload for a single operational concern (status, budget, gates, messages, actions, schedule). Part of the API decomposition standard.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Endpoint Schema
hasDefinedTerm.description: The expected JSON shape of an agent endpoint response. Defined per-endpoint in the API decomposition proposal. Compositor validates responses against these schemas.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Directive Tier
hasDefinedTerm.description: Enforcement level for a directive message. Three tiers: E0 advisory (recommendation, no obligation), E1 soft-mandatory (execute or escalate to human with rationale), E2 hard-mandatory (execute unconditionally, requires human co-signature). Modeled after HashiCorp Sentinel enforcement tiers.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Directive Scope
hasDefinedTerm.description: Capability attenuation for a mandatory directive. Declares allowed_paths (glob patterns for files the directive may modify), allowed_operations (categories: file-modify, config-change, process-restart, state-db-update), and prohibited_operations (explicit exclusions). Receiver validates directive body against scope before execution.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Admission Control
hasDefinedTerm.description: Receiver-side validation gate for incoming directives. Runs in autonomous-sync.sh before LLM evaluation phase. Checks: SSH signature validity, nonce uniqueness (replay protection), expiry, content hash integrity, sender role authorization, and scope compliance against local directive_policy. Modeled after Kubernetes admission controllers.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Neuroglial Layer
hasDefinedTerm.description: Infrastructure support layer in cogarch modeled after biological glial cells. Maps six glial cell types to mesh infrastructure functions: astrocyte (budget/routing), oligodendrocyte (caching), microglia (BFT/pruning), ependymal (maintenance), radial glia (bootstrap scaffolding), schwann (transport integrity). The glial agent maintains ambient state that domain agents consume without explicit request.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Ambient State
hasDefinedTerm.description: Mesh state maintained continuously by the operations-agent that domain agents consume passively — analogous to chemical gradients maintained by glial cells. Examples: mesh topology, vocabulary definitions, agent discovery endpoints, role verification status. Domain agents depend on ambient state without explicitly requesting its production.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Complement Cascade
hasDefinedTerm.description: Three-step pruning protocol modeled after microglial complement-mediated synaptic elimination (Schafer et al., 2012). Steps: C1q tag (flag inactive sessions >14 days), C3 verify (confirm no pending messages or open gates), phagocytose (archive to transport/archive/ and remove from sessions/). SHIP1 brake prevents over-pruning — never removes sessions with open gates or pending ACKs.

hasDefinedTerm.name: Trust Matrix
hasDefinedTerm.description: NxN matrix of trust scores across mesh agents, measured on 4 dimensions: availability (reachability), integrity (response validation), compliance (protocol adherence), epistemic honesty (uncertainty reporting). Scores use exponential moving average with charitable prior (1.0). Aggregate computed as geometric mean — penalizes weakness in any single dimension. Persisted in KV, accumulates across all observations.
